Motion To Compel Defendant Usa West Province Of The Society Of Jesus Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Number One
Off Calendar Apr-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT USA WEST PROVINCE OF THE SOCIETY OF JESUS' FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET NUMBER ONE

Appearances via Zoom:
Timothy Hale of Nye Stirling Hale Miller & Sweet, LLP (805.963.2345, tim@nshmlaw.com) for plaintiff and moving party;
Benjamin Lewis of Weintraub Tobin Chediak Coleman Grodin Law Corporation (415.433.1400, blewis@weintraub.com) for defendant USA West Province, Society of Jesus.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Matthew Wagner's motion to compel further responses to set one requests for admission from defendant USA West Province of Society of Jesus is granted in part. USA West must provide verified complete code-compliant further responses to requests for admissions 1-56 for the time period up to and including December 31, 1996, but not thereafter.

The parties dispute whether USA West's knowledge of accusations of sexual abuse allegedly committed by its employees and agents other than Peter Devine is discoverable. Both Wagner and USA West contend that Doe v. Roman Catholic Archbishop of Los Angeles (2021) 70 Cal. App. 5th 657 supports their position on this motion. In that case the court relied on evidence showing that the Los Angeles Archdiocese was aware prior to the alleged sexual abuse of the plaintiff that its clergy had been accused of sexually abusing other minors besides the plaintiff in determining that it was "reasonably foreseeable that minors attending ... classes in 1988 [the year that the plaintiff in that case he had been sexually abused] might be sexually molested by a priest, even though the Archdiocese did not have knowledge of prior sexual misconduct by Higson [the person who allegedly sexually abuse the plaintiff] specifically." (70 Cal. App. 5th at 676). The court's "reasonably foreseeable" determination was a significant factor in its holding that there was sufficient evidence of foreseeability of "whether the category of negligent conduct at issue is sufficiently likely to result in the kind of harm experienced" by the plaintiff to impose a duty on the Archdiocese to protect the plaintiff from sexual abuse by priests or other persons under the Archdiocese's control." Accordingly, this case strongly supports Wagner's position that USA West's knowledge of accusations of sexual abuse allegedly committed by its employees and agents other than Devine is discoverable.

Wagner seeks discovery of USA West's knowledge of sexual abuse accusations that were made after the alleged sexual abuse of Wagner in 1996. (See, e.g., requests 7 and 8). In Doe the court framed its foreseeability inquiry based on the knowledge of the Archdiocese as of the time of the alleged abuse of plaintiff. Therefore, this court determines that the discoverability period for USA West's knowledge of sexual abuse accusations of its employees and agents ends the last day of the year that Wagner alleges he was sexually abused by Devine.

Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Carol Lynn Cox, CSR 5128 (714.336.1430, carollynncox@aol.com) (301/HEK)

Motion To Compel Defendant Usa West Province Of The Society Of Jesus Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Number One
Off Calendar Apr-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT USA WEST PROVINCE OF THE SOCIETY OF JESUS' FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET NUMBER ONE

Appearances via Zoom:
Timothy Hale of Nye Stirling Hale Miller & Sweet, LLP (805.963.2345, tim@nshmlaw.com) for plaintiff and moving party;
Benjamin Lewis of Weintraub Tobin Chediak Coleman Grodin Law Corporation (415.433.1400, blewis@weintraub.com) for defendant USA West Province, Society of Jesus.

Argued and the court modifies its ruling as follows: Plaintiff Matthew Wagner's motion to compel further responses to his set one special interrogatories from defendant USA West Province of Society of Jesus is granted in part. USA West must provide verified complete code-compliant further responses to special interrogatories 1-8, 11-20, 115-119 and 122-123 for the time period January 1, 1974 through December 31, 1996, with the exception that documents dated after December 31, 1996 should be included to the extent they are related to accusations made before December 31, 1996. Because USA West agreed to provide further responses to interrogatories 9-10 and 27-30, those interrogatories are not part of this order.

The parties dispute whether USA West's knowledge of accusations of sexual abuse allegedly committed by its employees and agents other than Peter Devine is discoverable. Both Wagner and USA West contend that Doe v. Roman Catholic Archbishop of Los Angeles (2021) 70 Cal. App. 5th 657 supports their position on this motion. In that case the court relied on evidence showing that the Los Angeles Archdiocese was aware prior to the alleged sexual abuse of the plaintiff that its clergy had been accused of sexually abusing other minors besides the plaintiff in determining that it was "reasonably foreseeable that minors attending ... classes in 1988 [the year that the plaintiff in that case he had been sexually abused] might be sexually molested by a priest, even though the Archdiocese did not have knowledge of prior sexual misconduct by Higson [the person who allegedly sexually abuse the plaintiff] specifically." (70 Cal. App. 5th at 676). The court's "reasonably foreseeable" determination was a significant factor in its holding that there was sufficient evidence of foreseeability of "whether the category of negligent conduct at issue is sufficiently likely to result in the kind of harm experienced" by the plaintiff to impose a duty on the Archdiocese to protect the plaintiff from sexual abuse by priests or other persons under the Archdiocese's control." Accordingly, this case strongly supports Wagner's position that USA West's knowledge of accusations of sexual abuse allegedly committed by its employees and agents other than Devine is discoverable.

Wagner seeks discovery of USA West's knowledge of sexual abuse accusations from 1940 through the present, yet provides no basis for its 86 year discovery period and, in particular, provides no basis for the discoverability of accusations that were made after the alleged sexual abuse of Wagner in 1996. In Doe the court referred to the Archdiocese's knowledge of alleged sexual abuse that occurred roughly 21 years prior to the alleged abuse of the plaintiff and framed its foreseeability inquiry based on the knowledge of the Archdiocese as of the alleged abuse of plaintiff. Therefore, this court determines that the discoverability period for USA West's knowledge of sexual abuse accusations of its employees and agents begins the first day of the year Devine began teaching at SI and ends the last day of the year that Wagner alleges he was sexually abused by Devine.

Proposed order submitted. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Carol Lynn Cox, CSR 5128 (714.336.1430, carollynncox@aol.com) (301/HEK)
